title: Execute Commands in Pods
description:
## Execute Commands in Pods

Sometimes you need to run arbitrary commands inside a running container to debug or change state.

**Your task:**

A pod named `worker-pod` is running. Use `kubectl exec` to create an empty file at `/tmp/ready` inside the container.

```bash
# Verify the file was created:
kubectl exec worker-pod -- ls /tmp/ready
```

hints.title: Use kubectl exec
hints.body: You can pass commands to the container by adding `--` after the pod name.
